# Transcrição: 5_reasons_to_become_a_better_listener

- **Fonte:** 5_reasons_to_become_a_better_listener
- **Duração:** 01:23
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:23]

Here are the top five things that I keep in mind when I try to motivate myself to be a better listener. The first thing is, when you're speaking, you're just repeating what you know. When you're listening, that's when you're actually learning something new. The second thing, every single human being on this earth actually has a gold nugget that you can learn from, that if you actually dig for the gold nugget, it can change your life. The third reason, a big part of why we don't want to listen to other people is, often we're scared that other people might have an opposing view to the one we have. But imagine you lived in a world where everybody believes the same thing you believe and they believe you, you believe them, then you're essentially living in an echo chamber. Who wants to live in that kind of a world? This is why we need to listen, because the perspectives of other people actually matter, because they can see things you can't see. The fourth thing, here's a really good one, might I say so for myself. Most people in the world, they aren't listening, they're just waiting for their turn to speak. So imagine that. Imagine we live in a world where the majority of people are waiting for their turn to speak and no one's actually listening. Guess what? Your unique value proposition, you being able to be different, is you actually listening and then all of a sudden you're different to the 99% of others out there. And the fifth one and final one is when you listen, you're able to build more meaningful connections with other people. These are the top five things that I keep at the front of my mind to really keep me motivated to be a better listener.
